description_long: This shelter features a modern, minimalist design composed of two intersecting rectangular volumes, one clad in light wood and the other in smooth gray concrete. The structure includes a prominent gabled roof section with large, black-framed glass panels at each end, allowing natural light to flood the interior. A set of concrete steps leads up to a small platform, suggesting an elevated entryway. The wood-clad section has a small, rectangular window and a cantilevered platform, while the concrete section is more monolithic and windowless. The overall form is geometric and clean, with sharp lines and a clear distinction between the wood and concrete materials. The shelter appears suitable for use as a small urban pavilion, bus stop, or park structure.
description: A modern shelter with intersecting wood and concrete volumes, featuring gabled glass ends and a minimalist, geometric design.
description_view_0: Front view shows the juxtaposition of the wood and concrete volumes, the entry steps, and the cantilevered platform.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the gabled glass facade framed in black, with the concrete section in the foreground.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the wood-clad volume with a small window and the cantilevered platform, with the concrete block behind.
description_view_3: Opposite gabled end view reveals the wood-clad glass facade and the platform, with the concrete section receding.
attribute_roof_type: gabled roof with wood cladding and concrete slab sections
attribute_entryway_design: stepped entry with a small platform and large glass doors
attribute_support_structure: reinforced concrete walls and wood-framed gabled roof
attribute_transparency: partially transparent glass panels at both gabled ends
